title: 3DMark

notes: Had to explicitly choose 32 or 64bit.   FireStrike runs well with no problems, but can't detect your system info.  Can't run TimeSpy becuase it doesn't detect a 64bit OS.
title: 3DMark

notes: DX9, 10 and 11 tests work fine. Application won't start with wine set to win10 mode, hence I couldn't test DX12 tests with vkd3d. System information won't be detected and system info scanner needs to either be disabled in settings or killed each time you quit the game. You can send results online by having connected Steam account, though for the advanced key to work you need to add it in HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\UL\3DMark and "KeyCode" string with your key from Steam as value.
title: 3DMark

notes: Runs well on Ryzen 7 1700X. 3DMark doesn't recognise that I own it, though. Only basic edition features are available.
title: 3DMark

notes: You need to force 32-bit or 64-bit to get it to launch. Also, you need to add the CD key to the registry (as described in the proton issue) to get 3DMark Advanced. The Direct3D 12 tests are not working yet. Performance is not as high as it is on Windows.
title: 3DMark

notes: You need to downgrade to 4.2-9. It is broken otherwise. Also, you need to force 32-bit or 64-bit to get it to launch. Also, you need to add the CD key to the registry (as described in the proton issue) to get 3DMark Advanced. The Direct3D 12 tests are not working yet. Performance is not as high as it is on Windows.
title: 3DMark

notes: By default, it doesn't run.You have to lauch the 32 or 64 bit version and use protontricks to manually add your CDKEY to the game with a 'protontricks -c 'wine cmd /C reg add "HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Futuremark\3DMark" /v "KeyCode" /t REG_SZ /d "<CDKEY>"' 223850'. After that it works pretty well.
title: 3DMark

notes: Besides having to manually edit the register it runs perfect. I posted a steamcommunity video at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rw2C1La33Wk&feature=youtu.be
title: 3DMark
